title: wahlenbergia planiflora subsp. longipila
intro: Wahlenbergia planiflora subsp. longipila, also known as the long-stalked bluebell, is a species of flowering plant in the Campanulaceae family. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, particularly in traditional African medicine, where it is used to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which provide health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, Wahlenbergia planiflora subsp. longipila is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
